primary_icp.description: People building or running marketplaces who want help with operations, growth, and support.
evidence: Homepage: "Do 10x more on your marketplace with the team you have."
Homepage: "The first AI super agent for marketplaces."

evidence: Homepage: "growth experiments"
Homepage: "Runs your ads" and "Wins repeat business"

secondary_audiences.value: marketplace support and trust & safety teams

audience_notes.value: The site consistently addresses marketplace operators and founders; it also references specialist functions like growth, support, disputes, and engineering as parts of the workflow, not separate buyer segments.
evidence: Homepage: "one super agent. The whole marketplace lifecycle."
Homepage: "Behind the scenes it runs a crew of specialist agents for marketing, disputes, support, and engineering."

buyer_pains.pain: Marketplace teams have too few people to handle many operational functions.
buyer_pains.description: The site emphasizes doing much more with the team you already have.
evidence: Homepage: "Do 10x more on your marketplace with the team you have."

buyer_pains.pain: Marketplace operations are fragmented across build, growth, support, and dispute workflows.
buyer_pains.description: The company positions one agent as covering the whole marketplace lifecycle instead of separate tools for each slice.
evidence: Homepage: "Most tools do one slice. Zavi does the whole arc"
About: "Not another tool to manage."

buyer_pains.pain: Seller supply needs constant recruiting, verification, and activation to avoid empty listings.
buyer_pains.description: The platform claims to onboard supply so listings do not run dry.
evidence: Homepage: "Onboards supply" and "Recruits, verifies, and activates new sellers so your listings never run dry."

buyer_pains.pain: Marketplace growth requires ongoing ad optimization and repeat-business experiments.
buyer_pains.description: The copy points to managing paid campaigns, retention, and funnel leak fixes as recurring pain points.
evidence: Homepage: "Runs your ads"
Homepage: "Wins repeat business"
Homepage: "Plugs the leaks"

buyer_pains.pain: Operators need to keep support, disputes, and escalations moving continuously.
buyer_pains.description: The site frames around-the-clock operations as necessary for marketplaces.
buyer_pains.audience_segment: marketplace support and trust & safety teams
evidence: Homepage: "Run24/7"
Pricing: credits apply across "seller onboarding, fraud reviews, disputes, and operator escalations"
About: "keep ops, support, and disputes moving"

positioning_summary.value: Zavi positions itself as an AI super agent built specifically for marketplaces, replacing many fragmented tools with one autonomous system that builds, grows, and runs the marketplace.
evidence: Homepage: "The first AI super agent for marketplaces."
Homepage: "Most tools do one slice. Zavi does the whole arc"
About: "Not another tool to manage."

primary_differentiators.description: It focuses on marketplace operations rather than generic automation.
evidence: Homepage: "marketplace ops"
Homepage: "The first AI super agent for marketplaces."

primary_differentiators.description: Claims to cover build, growth, and run phases in one system.
evidence: Homepage: "Build0 → 1"
Homepage: "Grow1 → n"
Homepage: "Run24/7"

primary_differentiators.description: A single front-end agent coordinates specialist agents for different functions.
evidence: Homepage: "Behind the scenes it runs a crew of specialist agents for marketing, disputes, support, and engineering."

primary_differentiators.description: The agent works in Slack threads and mentions where work already happens.
evidence: Homepage: "Meet your AI teammate, live in Slack"
Pricing: "Slack-native agent in threads + mentions"

primary_differentiators.description: Pricing is tied to credits and model usage rather than per-seat licensing.
evidence: Pricing: "Credits are the fuel behind every action Zavi takes."
Pricing: "No per-seat licenses, no minimums"

category_point_of_view.value: Marketplace growth and operations should be handled by an always-on AI system that coordinates specialist work, not by separate point tools or manual operator effort.
evidence: Homepage: "Most tools do one slice. Zavi does the whole arc"
Homepage: "One super agent. The whole marketplace lifecycle."
About: "A super agent doing the listings, campaigns, support, and fixes while you sleep."

positioning_notes.value: The site repeatedly uses AI-speed, autonomous execution, and marketplace lifecycle language to frame Zavi as an operator replacement/augmentation layer for marketplaces.
evidence: About: "The future belongs to whoever moves at AI speed."
Homepage: "you ask, Zavi acts, the outcome lands."
Start page: "Zavi thinks, builds, and markets your projects autonomously."
Homepage: "Do 10x more on your marketplace with the team you have."
About: "Move at AI speed."
Homepage: "you ask, Zavi acts, the outcome lands."

writing_style.value: Punchy, declarative, and outcome-led, with short headline fragments and repeated lifecycle metaphors.
evidence: Homepage: "Build0 → 1 / Grow1 → n / Run24/7"
Homepage: "One super agent. The whole marketplace lifecycle."
About: "Not another tool to manage."

evidence: Homepage: "The first AI super agent for marketplaces."
Pricing: "Run your marketplace with a super agent that works 24/7."

evidence: Homepage: "Zavi builds, grows, and runs your marketplace"

Homepage: "Run24/7"
Pricing: "Run your marketplace with a super agent thatworks 24/7."

common_phrases.context: Used in the about page to contrast the product with manual workflows.
evidence: About: "The future belongs to whoever moves at AI speed."
About: "Move at AI speed."

messaging_patterns.pattern: Pairs broad promise with concrete marketplace functions such as onboarding, disputes, ads, and support.
messaging_patterns.description: The marketing alternates between high-level category claims and specific operational tasks.
evidence: Homepage: "seller onboarding, fraud and disputes, growth experiments, and daily operator updates"
Homepage: "Runs your ads"

messaging_patterns.pattern: Frames the product as a replacement for many tools and many roles.
messaging_patterns.description: The copy emphasizes one agent and a crew behind it instead of point solutions.
evidence: Homepage: "Most tools do one slice. Zavi does the whole arc"
Homepage: "one super agent, a whole crew behind it"

messaging_patterns.pattern: Emphasizes user control through approvals and dashboards.
messaging_patterns.description: Autonomy is balanced with human approval for bigger actions.
evidence: Homepage: "You approve the big moves from one dashboard."
Homepage: "Approve money and message moves before they fire"

messaging_patterns.pattern: Uses credit-based pricing language to connect usage with action volume.
messaging_patterns.description: Pricing copy explains what credits do and how they map to work.
evidence: Pricing: "Credits power everything Zavi does"
Pricing: "Pay for what your super agent does"

missing_information: Specific customer examples beyond marketplaces are not provided.
Company size, founding date, and broader stage signals are not fully stated.
Integrations are listed, but the exact depth of each integration is not described.
Detailed pricing tiers beyond Team and Enterprise are not shown in the crawl.
potential_risks: The crawl contains several mock Slack/dashboard examples; these should not be mistaken for separate product categories or customer segments.
Some pages repeat similar hero and CTA copy, which may overstate message frequency slightly.
The product is described with strong autonomous language, but the exact scope of autonomy and human approval boundaries are not fully detailed.
profile_quality.notes: The crawl provides strong, consistent evidence for a marketplace-specific AI operations product with clear positioning, pricing, and core workflow claims. Evidence quality is good, with limited ambiguity primarily around maturity and exact feature boundaries.
